primary_job: Give a short, personal, visual explanation with less scheduling friction than a live call.
best_for: Seasonal output or roof/shade visual explanation
Light logistics when WhatsApp opt-in exists
bad_for: Customer has requested written documents only
Trust is damaged by perceived informality
Claims requiring detailed sources and tables
timing_guidance: Use when a visual explanation can replace a scheduling-heavy call.
Keep under 90 seconds for the demo.
Use after the customer asks a specific question or after a call/visit recap.
Do not use for repeated no-response unless there is a strong value-add and opt-in.
prep_needed: Claim checks for any numbers mentioned
tone_guidance: Show the exact part of the quote or chart being discussed.
End with a simple reply option.

video_structures.name: Battery now vs later
beats: Explain when it helps and when later-ready is enough.
Name the one missing decision input.

video_structures.name: Partner-shareable recap
beats: Say this is a short recap for both decision-makers.
Summarize top financial or impact assumption.

video_structures.name: Seasonal output explanation
beats: State what data would improve confidence.
Offer call or visit if needed.

success_indicators: Customer books a call or visit
Customer asks for final recap or contract
debrief_questions: What exact question did it answer?
Was it shared with another stakeholder?
guardrails: Do not use WhatsApp without opt-in.
Do not include sensitive finance or legal claims casually in video.
Do not rely on video views unless tracking consent exists.
Do not make the video generic; it must reference the customer's situation.
